programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: Here's a tongue twister: Theresa, tuna, tangerine! In this episode, host Theresa Visintin picks her favourite letter, "T," and introduces us to scrumptious recipes using tuna and tangerine. Theresa loves tuna and Italian cuisine, so naturally she combines both to create a mouth-watering pasta with some savoury tomato, olive, and tuna sauce. Next, Theresa satisfies her sweet tooth with a decadent tangerine parfait! Using the sweet juice of the tangerine, Theresa creates gelatin, with a hint of orange liqueur. She tops it all off by whipping up a fluffy cream to complete this sweet treat. Then it's time for a real challenge: tuna and tangerine in one recipe!

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: In this "H" challenge, Chef Lauren Gulyas decides to take on the unique flavours of halibut and horseradish. She starts with a perfectly prepared prosciutto wrapped halibut, pairing the buttery flavour of the fish with the saltiness of the cured meat. Throw in some dill potatoes and you've got a beautifully balanced dish. Next up, Chef Lauren show us how to make a delightful roast beef dip with a zesty horseradish sauce using carrots, vinegar, mayo and a little lemon. Finally, she is challenged to make a dish that combines both ingredients, and does she ever deliver!

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: In this letter "P" challenge, Chef Robert Jewell showcases his vegetarian skills using pecans and peppers. Despite his cowboy roots, Robert shows us he can kick it with vegetarians by cooking up a juicy meatless "meatloaf" using pecans. But, no "meatloaf" would be complete without a gravy! Robert fries up earthy portobello mushrooms before adding a special ingredient to thicken the gravy, you guessed it -- pecans! Now, on to peppers. Robert doubles up, using both the vegetable and seasoning, to make a delicious red pepper coulis with herb and pepper crusted beef tenderloin. Robert goes a little nuts when he prepares his final recipe using both pecans and peppers.

programme.desc: Chef Dev shows off street food recipes that use less common cuts of meat, including Fish Head Curry from Singapore, Pork and Pâté Banh Mi from Vietnam, and Jamaican Jerk Oxtail.

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: Cheese or chocolate? Why not both! In this "C" challenge, host Theresa Visintin takes on both decadent ingredients. But first, she shows us that being cheesy is easy, especially when you stuff chicken breast with creamy goat's cheese and finely diced apples. Theresa takes the flavour one step further by coating the stuffed chicken with sage, parmesan, and panko bread crumbs, then frying it to a crispy golden brown. Next, Theresa whips up a dessert fan favourite, the decadent and oh-so-irresistible dark chocolate lava cake. Finally, Theresa has an irresistible recipe using ooey gooey cheese and warm chocolate.

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Appreciating island culture, host Michael Bonacini explores the enchanting region of Sardegna and its exceptional cuisine. Michael begins his island feast with a taste of the sea through his seasoned antipasto of Stuffed Snails. Next, Michael prepares a luxurious and delicate Lobster Spaghetti, highlighting the fresh seafood of the region. Moving inland for his secondo, Michael cooks up a traditional dish of Lamb with Artichokes and Saffron, a meal that combines three of Sardegna's most famous ingredients. To end his meal, Michael combines salty and sweet with a dolce of Honey and Cheese Fritters.

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: One of Italy's quieter regions, Friuli Venezia Guilia offers iconic decadent dishes, waiting to be discovered. Renowned chef Michael Bonacini begins his exploration of the region with his take on a traditional meal: Cheese Crisp Baskets with Polenta and Mushrooms. This savoury antipasto is an elegant looking dish with humble flavours. For his primo, Michael prepares a unique Poppy Seed, Sage, and Prosciutto Pasta, a dish that is a twist on traditional pasta. Looking for a little heat in his secondo, Michael cooks up a meal of Spicy Tomato Scampi. The sweet meat of the scampi counters the heat of the sauce, creating a delicious flavour combination. To end his meal, Michael looks to the...

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Chef Michael Bonacini uses the fresh ingredients from rich Tuscan soil to make regional dishes like Spinach Ricotta Gnudi.

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Celebrity Chef Michael Bonacini explores classic dishes from the central Italian region, Le Marche.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: China celebrates harvest with the Moon Festival, when the moon is at its fullest during mid-autumn. The best part? A delicious feast! Cantonese expert Mary Tang prepares a common Moon Festival dish - earthy and tasty Mushroom Stir Fry. Then, she cooks up a mouth-watering, Cantonese must-have - Steamed Crab. Using flawless technique, Mary breaks the juicy crab into pieces, fries and steams it in scented spices and sauces. Another Moon Festival staple is Cantonese Roast Duck - Mary uses sweet-savoury Chinese five-spice powder, dark soy sauce, and aromatic white pepper to make a fragrant rub. Finally, Moon Cake at the Moon Festival a given. Exquisitely crafted with red bean paste and sweet...

programme.title: Pressure's On
programme.desc: Who has "thyme" to make dinner after a hectic day? Host Vanessa Gianfrancesco inspires with four recipes including a Mushroom Thyme Risotto.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: It's all about comfort food as Japanese-German chef and cookbook author Hana Etsuko Dethlefsen fixes up chicken hotpot, chicken skewers, meat and potato, and a sweet custard.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: If you have 30 minutes, then you have time to make Chinese take-out dinner for four people! Chef Spencer Watts cooks up a feast that costs $30.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Thai chef and cookbook author Pailin Chongchitnant shows us how to make Thai dishes that were inspired by other cultures, from steamed dumplings to biryani to Massaman curry and custard tarts.

programme.title: Let's Brunch
programme.desc: Charming host Vanessa is your host for this Dim Sum episode -- try your hand at dumplings, congee and more!

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Host Jessica McGovern loves the flavours of fall so she bakes up some delicious treats using warm, autumn inspired ingredients.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: What should you do in a wild winter blizzard? Bake! Host Jessica McGovern makes sweet winter treats like an extravagant Gingerbread Chalet.

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: For this "N" challenge, Chef Lauren Gulyas works with noodles and nectarines. For her first dish, Lauren cooks up a breaded nectarine and mozzarella stack. Talking technique, she shows us how to cut nectarines into rounds. She does the same with some soft mozzarella and then walks us through the art of a balsamic reduction. For the noodles, Chef Lauren puts an elegant twist on your childhood favourite with a lobster mac and cheese. She spares no flavours, adding fried bacon, garlic, cayenne pepper, mustard powder, and even dry vermouth. This is no ordinary noodle dish. But now for the real challenge! Chef Lauren works with the noodles and nectarines.

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: In this episode, Chef Robert Jewell explores recipes using two very different ingredients that start with the letter "O," oats and okra. For his first dish, Robert uses okra in a classic cornmeal cake recipe, but bumps up the flavour factor with spicy jalapenos. Robert balances the heat of the jalapeno with a tart green tomato and crunchy red onion salsa. In his next recipe, Robert keeps the heat, but switches up his recipes for a curry-spiced oat pancake. This Indian inspired dish needs something to cool it down and Robert knows exactly what that is - sweet, silky applesauce! He's up for the challenge when it comes to combining okra and oats into one dish, and the results are tasty!

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: Culinary expert Theresa Visintin picks the letter "B" and must combine beef with buttermilk for her challenge. But first, she works with the ingredients separately. Silky buttermilk lends itself perfectly to Theresa's battered blooming onion recipe. Using the biggest onion she can find, Theresa skillfully slices from the root down, ensuring it will bloom into a beautiful flower. She coats the Vidalia onion in egg, buttermilk and smoky spiced flour, before deep frying it. Next up, it's time for beef to get the Theresa treatment, as she prepares a crowd favourite- smoky, juicy, fall-off-thebone baked beef brisket. With the perfect balance of sweet and spicy, this meal will make you look...

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: China celebrates harvest with the Moon Festival, when the moon is at its fullest during mid-autumn. The best part? A delicious feast! Cantonese expert Mary Tang prepares a common Moon Festival dish - earthy and tasty Mushroom Stir Fry. Then, she cooks up a mouth-watering, Cantonese must-have - Steamed Crab. Using flawless technique, Mary breaks the juicy crab into pieces, fries and steams it in scented spices and sauces. Another Moon Festival staple is Cantonese Roast Duck - Mary uses sweet-savoury Chinese five-spice powder, dark soy sauce, and aromatic white pepper to make a fragrant rub. Finally, Moon Cake at the Moon Festival a given. Exquisitely crafted with red bean paste and sweet...

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: After picking "M" for this episode's challenge, Chef Lauren Gulyas decides to make meal magic using maple and mahi-mahi. She starts by combining chestnut and quinoa to create a mouth-watering medley, rich in flavour and texture. Chef Lauren adds shiitake mushrooms and drizzles the dish with a balsamic vinegar, maple syrup and rosemary dressing. Next up, she steams Mahi-mahi with red chilies and lemongrass, this savoury spice is a heavenly combination with an invigorating aroma. For the challenge, Chef Lauren seamlessly introduces both maple and mahimahi into one flavourful fusion.

programme.title: Cook Like a Chef

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: Here's a tongue twister: Theresa, tuna, tangerine! In this episode, host Theresa Visintin picks her favourite letter, "T," and introduces us to scrumptious recipes using tuna and tangerine. Theresa loves tuna and Italian cuisine, so naturally she combines both to create a mouth-watering pasta with some savoury tomato, olive, and tuna sauce. Next, Theresa satisfies her sweet tooth with a decadent tangerine parfait! Using the sweet juice of the tangerine, Theresa creates gelatin, with a hint of orange liqueur. She tops it all off by whipping up a fluffy cream to complete this sweet treat. Then it's time for a real challenge: tuna and tangerine in one recipe!

programme.title: Voices
programme.desc: Le voci delle aziende del mondo della moda, del beauty, del lifestyle sono le protagoniste del format. Interviste esclusive per raccontare la storia, i valori, i progetti e le novità di realtà dei grandi brand del settore

programme.title: Top Spots: Îles de la Madeleine
programme.desc: Discovering picturesque scenery from the unique Îles de la Madeleine.

programme.title: Tropical Weightlessness
programme.desc: Gently sway in the tropical turquoise waters with a glimpse of blue sky and palm trees.

programme.title: Earth From Above
programme.desc: Traveling the world with a birds-eye-view perspective of scenic wonders.

programme.title: Nature's Beauty: World of Relaxation
programme.desc: Scenes from over 100 different exotic, natural destinations around the world; a seamless, graciously flowing mix of several Nature Relaxation shorter length productions, designed to keep you in a state of complete relaxation from start to finish; transitioning you from golden sunsets to snowy mountaintops; from hidden canyon waterfalls to tropical islands so pure you've likely never seen anything like it!

programme.title: Whiritoa Beach, New Zealand
programme.desc: Discover the raw beauty of this secluded New Zealand beach, surrounded by wildflowers.

programme.title: Flying Over the Atlantic Ocean
programme.desc: Travelling the coasts of the Atlantic Ocean from a bird's-eye view.

programme.title: Shark Paradise
programme.desc: Captured by filmmaker collaborator Peter Schneider, Shark Paradise features hundreds of unique real-time scenes captured from around the world, paired with gentle underwater sounds and calming music for deep relaxation by Relax Moods.

programme.title: SereneScapes
programme.desc: Feeling the wondrous expanse of a spectacular desert surrounded by towering mountain peaks.

programme.title: Ninety Mile Beach: New Zealand
programme.desc: The almost never-ending paradise of one of New Zealand's most breathtaking beaches.

programme.title: Wells Gray Falls
programme.desc: A peaceful soundtrack with a waterfall tumbling over the edge of a cliff.

programme.title: Benang Kelambu & Stokel: Indonesia
programme.desc: Experience a waterfall flowing down a forest wall, surrounded by overgrown vines.

programme.title: Wharariki Beach I: New Zealand
programme.desc: Exploring the sandy shores and rocky cliffs of a majestic beach in New Zealand.

programme.title: Sparking Patagonia Waves
programme.desc: A cool, refreshing window to the glacial lakeshore of Patagonia's Torres Del Paine National Park, in Chile, all without having to fly 20+ hours, then backpack 20+ miles.

programme.title: Spirit Lake Relaxation
programme.desc: Relax for 3 hours at the gorgeous Spirit Lake, in Mount St Helen's Washington with this soothing dynamic nature film containing only the pure sounds of Nature!

programme.title: Tarawera Lake & Taupo: New Zealand
programme.desc: Various nature scenery videos.

programme.title: Above the Tulip Fields
programme.desc: Tulip fields as seen from a drone perspective in the windmill-filled country of Holland.

programme.title: Beachside Hut: Dominican Republic
programme.desc: Golden sand, crystal-clear waters, infinite blue sky, and a charming hut on the Dominican Republic's Playa Coson.

programme.title: Beautiful Kauai: Hawaii
programme.desc: Kauai's natural beauty.

programme.title: Nacpan Beach
programme.desc: Travelling to a beautiful stretch of cream-colored sand with swaying coconut palms and clear waters.

programme.title: Atuh Beach: Indonesia
programme.desc: Take a tour of beautiful Indonesian waters, surrounded by impressive rock formations.

programme.title: New Zealand Gardens
programme.desc: The filmmaker explores the breath-taking gardens of New Zealand and provides the diversity and beauty of these enchanting havens.

programme.title: Ulun Danu Temple: Indonesia
programme.desc: Feel the tranquility of an ancient temple and the serene views of its surrounding waters.

programme.title: Rugged Coast: Newfoundland
programme.desc: The solidarity peacefulness of the Fort Point lighthouse on Newfoundland and Labrador's rugged coast.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland
programme.desc: An aerial journey to the remote fjords and valleys of eastern Iceland.

programme.title: Abel Tasman National Park
programme.desc: The lush coastal native bush of the Abel Tasman National Park in New Zealand.

programme.title: Tumpak Sewu Waterfalls: East Java
programme.desc: A close look at the edge of the tiered, plunging streams of Indonesia's Tumpak Sewu falls.

programme.title: Fly Over Kelingking Beach: Indonesia
programme.desc: A bird's eye view of a scenic beach surrounded by pristine waters and high cliffs.

programme.title: Pancake Rocks: New Zealand
programme.desc: New Zealand's Pancake Rocks offer a mesmerizing combination of geology, natural beauty, and coastal splendor, with intricate rock patterns and shapes formed over millions of years of wind and water exposure.

programme.title: A Day in Paradise
programme.desc: Filmed in 2013 by David Huting, the tiny island is hidden off of the northeastern coast of the outer Fiji Islands (near Taveuni), and boasts a perfect white sandy beach, groves of palm trees, and a majestic lagoon that shelters the island from all sides. It has more turtle visitors than humans!

programme.title: Glacial Beauty: Newfoundland
programme.desc: Admiring the quiet and glacial beauty of icebergs drifting across the bay at Newfoundland's Amherst Cove.

programme.title: Rainbow Reef Relaxation 2
programme.desc: Vibrant underwater reefs, including many scenes which no longer look as depicted due to coral bleaching.

programme.title: Kapas Biru Waterfall: Indonesia
programme.desc: Escape to Indonesia with tropical vegetation and a rushing waterfall.

programme.title: Nusa Penida: Indonesia
programme.desc: A bird's eye view of Nusa Penida.

programme.title: Wildlife Wonders of South America
programme.desc: A sunrise at Salar de Uyuni in Bolivia with flamingo and llama scenes from high altitude volcanic salt lakes; welcoming new life in the Chilean landscapes of San Pedro Atacama; the Torres del Paine National Park in Patagonia.

programme.title: Beach Paradise: Hawaii
programme.desc: Spending a restful moment in Maui, a tropical paradise of gleaming white sand beaches and turquoise waters.

programme.title: White Beach: Philippines
programme.desc: Lazing the day away on a white sand beach in the Philippines as the sun gently sets on the horizon.

programme.title: Spectacular Setting Sun
programme.desc: The sun sets as it sinks behind the mountains of the Laurentians, Quebec.

programme.title: Sunset Sierra Creek
programme.desc: A golden window to the Sierra mountains.

programme.title: Thermal Wonderland: New Zealand
programme.desc: Free from rabbit holes but still full of magic, Waiotapu Thermal Wonderland has an impressive geothermal landscape.

programme.title: America From Above
programme.desc: Part one of a new series of ambient drone films for relaxation, America From Above is a spectacular yet ultra-soothing 1-hour long natural journey that sweeps viewers gently above America's vistas. You'll begin the farmlands of Iowa, soaring west through the Rocky Mountains of Colorado, then on to Utah's iconic red rock landscapes and finally ending on the West Coast with a familiar voyage over Big Sur and then back to San Diego just in time for sunset.

programme.title: Boardwalk to the Beach: Guadeloupe
programme.desc: Strolling on the boardwalk to the turquoise waters in Le Gosier, Guadeloupe.

programme.title: Breezy Beach: Dominican Republic
programme.desc: Discovering the dazzling sapphire waters on the Dominican Republic's Playa Las Galeras.

programme.title: Return to Paradise 1
programme.desc: Featuring the hidden exotic Fijian island of Nanuku, a tiny, palm-covered, sandy atoll surrounded by lagoons brimming with sea life; some aerial drone clips give perspective from above.

programme.title: A Day on the Napali Coast
programme.desc: An ambient nature film features the spectacular sights and sounds of the legendary 11-mile Kalalau Trail on the Hawaiian island of Kauai.

programme.title: North Island's Waterfalls: New Zealand
programme.desc: The North Island region of New Zealand is home to several stunning waterfalls, from tall streams that snake over towering cliffsides to short yet wide plunges of cascading waters.

programme.title: Tropical Beach: Dominican Republic
programme.desc: A view of Playa Madama, an unspoiled beach framed by high bluffs at the edge of the Dominican Republic.

programme.title: Outdoor Fire
programme.desc: A cozy outdoor fire with breathtaking views of a snowy lake and forest.

programme.title: Fly Over Kauai, Hawaii
programme.desc: Experiencing Hawaii from above in a spectacular flight over stunning coastlines and lush landscapes.

programme.title: Beach Paradise: East Bali
programme.desc: A serene soundtrack amidst a journey to spectacular beaches in Bali.

programme.title: The Deep Med
programme.desc: Laurent Ballesta has yet again challenged himself to a new world record: spend 28 days at a depth of 100 meters, pushing the limits of his body to reveal the luxuriant and unknown depths of the Mediterranean.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: In Milan we'll take a peek at Italy's highest fashion, fanciest delis, grandest cemetery, and greatest opera house...not to mention Leonardo's "Last Supper." Then we'll cruise Lake Como, settling down in the lakeside village of Varenna.

programme.title: Outdoor and Nature
programme.desc: OUTDOOR & Nature, a weekly program that highlights sports, outdoor activities, and the breathtaking scenery of Cyprus, will introduce you to the great outdoors. Each episode highlights government initiatives, community organisations, and volunteer efforts to preserve Cyprus's natural heritage while examining nature, wildlife, and conservation. Take a trip of inspiration, exploration, and adventure with hosts Panicos Pilalis and Aristo Aristeidou. Listen and get motivated to go on your own outdoor adventure in Cyprus!

programme.title: Gold Cuisine
programme.desc: Celebrate rustic baking in this episode, starting with a flavorful olive pie cake and finishing with charming mini olive pies sprinkled with sesame seeds, perfect for sharing or snacking.

programme.title: Brač (Supetar)
programme.desc: On Brač, famed for its white stone, Zlatni Rat beach, and Vidova Gora, we stopped in Supetar to taste tradition. Restaurateur Teo Dragičević served us šporka juha, mišanca, and vitalac—authentic island flavors rooted in Brač’s rich culinary heritage.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Palermo
programme.desc: Malini Angelica returns to Sicily to explore Palermo, Mondello, and Agrigento. From Palermo’s vibrant markets and rich history to Mondello’s seaside charm and the ancient Greek temples of Agrigento, she uncovers the island’s unique mix of cultures, cuisine, and character that make Sicily truly one of a kind.

programme.title: Outdoor and Nature
programme.desc: OUTDOOR & Nature, a weekly program that highlights sports, outdoor activities, and the breathtaking scenery of Cyprus, will introduce you to the great outdoors. Each episode highlights government initiatives, community organisations, and volunteer efforts to preserve Cyprus's natural heritage while examining nature, wildlife, and conservation. Take a trip of inspiration, exploration, and adventure with hosts Panicos Pilalis and Aristo Aristeidou. Listen and get motivated to go on your own outdoor adventure in Cyprus!

programme.title: The Odyssey Twilight of the Gods
programme.desc: After 20 years of war and wandering, Ulysses finally returns to Ithaca, where he reclaims his throne and reunites with his faithful wife, Penelope.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: Join Rick as we explore Andalucía - prancing horses, whitewashed hilltowns, kite surfing on sunny beaches. Then we'll summit the Rock of Gibraltar and sample Morocco, from tribal musicians and busy artisans to the magic of its markets

programme.title: Concerto per Milano 2022
programme.desc: The Filarmonica della Scala and principal conductor Riccardo Chailly return to Piazza Duomo for the evocative Concerto per Milano. A landmark cultural event, this open-air performance brings world-class music to the heart of the city. Television direction by Stefania Grimaldi captures the grandeur and emotion of the evening.

programme.title: Outdoor and Nature
programme.desc: OUTDOOR & Nature, a weekly program that highlights sports, outdoor activities, and the breathtaking scenery of Cyprus, will introduce you to the great outdoors. Each episode highlights government initiatives, community organisations, and volunteer efforts to preserve Cyprus's natural heritage while examining nature, wildlife, and conservation. Take a trip of inspiration, exploration, and adventure with hosts Panicos Pilalis and Aristo Aristeidou. Listen and get motivated to go on your own outdoor adventure in Cyprus!

programme.title: Gold Cuisine
programme.desc: Delve into wholesome flavors with a traditional chortopita, a savory pie filled with fresh greens and herbs, paired with a hearty chickpeas and spinach dish brightened with lemon and tomato for a satisfying, nutritious meal.

programme.title: Kaštela (U masliniku)
programme.desc: In Kaštela, a cluster of seven towns near Split, winemaking runs deep. Among olive groves and vineyards, Mladen Kuzmanić treated us to local flavors—sipa i bob, pancetta, goat cheese, and homemade sausage—perfectly paired with a glass of regional wine.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Calabria
programme.desc: Malini explores Calabria, a hidden gem in Southern Italy. While its coastal towns like Tropea and Scilla boast turquoise seas, Calabria’s true charm lies in its rural interior, where traditions like handmade pasta, cheese, and salami are still alive. She visits ghost villages like Pentedattilo in the Aspromonte mountains and experiences authentic Calabrian culture, food, and hospitality, hosted by Calabria Etnica.

programme.title: Concerto per Milano 2022
programme.desc: The Filarmonica della Scala and principal conductor Riccardo Chailly return to Piazza Duomo for the evocative Concerto per Milano. A landmark cultural event, this open-air performance brings world-class music to the heart of the city. Television direction by Stefania Grimaldi captures the grandeur and emotion of the evening.

programme.title: Elain Dutton: Agents of Change Panel
programme.desc: Building in the Mediterranean today is about more than beauty-it’s about survival and legacy. This panel, Agents of Change, spotlights the developers, architects, and planners driving resilient, sustainable, and climate-ready projects that will shape the region for generations.

programme.title: Kaštela (U masliniku)
programme.desc: In Kaštela, a cluster of seven towns near Split, winemaking runs deep. Among olive groves and vineyards, Mladen Kuzmanić treated us to local flavors—sipa i bob, pancetta, goat cheese, and homemade sausage—perfectly paired with a glass of regional wine.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: In Milan we'll take a peek at Italy's highest fashion, fanciest delis, grandest cemetery, and greatest opera house...not to mention Leonardo's "Last Supper." Then we'll cruise Lake Como, settling down in the lakeside village of Varenna.

programme.title: Jesenice
programme.desc: In the hillside village of Staro Selo Jesenice, with views of the central Dalmatian islands, we found stone houses, a bocce court, and a traditional tavern run by Ante Bajso. There, he treated us to a Dalmatian classic—meat slow-cooked under a bell-shaped lid, peka.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Rome
programme.desc: Malini Angelica explores Rome, Italy — a city of ancient ruins, grand monuments, and unforgettable food. She experiences daily Roman life, from enjoying cappuccino and cornetto at a local café to tasting classic pastas like cacio e pepe and pasta alla gricia. She visits local markets, enjoys a picnic at Villa Doria Pamphili, and discovers Rome’s iconic landmarks — the Spanish Steps, Trevi Fountain, and Pantheon — beautifully lit at night.

programme.title: PUCCINI TU TU PICCOLO ADDIO
programme.desc: In the final moments of Madama Butterfly, Cio-Cio-San sings "Tu, tu, piccolo Iddio", a tender farewell to her son before making the ultimate sacrifice.

programme.title: Panel Tokenizing Real Estate
programme.desc: Tokenization is transforming real estate by turning property into digital assets that can be bought, sold, and shared with unprecedented flexibility. This panel explores how blockchain enables fractional ownership, improves liquidity, and opens property investment to a wider, global audience—while addressing the legal, regulatory, and trust challenges shaping the future of tokenized real estate in the Mediterranean and beyond.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: We'll explore flamboyant Sevilla, where orange trees ring lacy palaces, bullfighting is still a cultural event, and little girls dream of growing up to become flamenco dancers.

programme.title: Outdoor and Nature EP8
programme.desc: OUTDOOR & Nature, a weekly program that highlights sports, outdoor activities, and the breathtaking scenery of Cyprus, will introduce you to the great outdoors. Each episode highlights government initiatives, community organisations, and volunteer efforts to preserve Cyprus's natural heritage while examining nature, wildlife, and conservation. Take a trip of inspiration, exploration, and adventure with hosts Panicos Pilalis and Aristo Aristeidou. Listen and get motivated to go on your own outdoor adventure in Cyprus!

programme.title: Gold Cuisine
programme.desc: Delve into wholesome flavors with a traditional chortopita, a savory pie filled with fresh greens and herbs, paired with a hearty chickpeas and spinach dish brightened with lemon and tomato for a satisfying, nutritious meal.

programme.title: Imotska krajina
programme.desc: Imotski, known for its stunning Red and Blue Lakes, draws nature lovers and active travelers alike. During a local marenda break, host Jurica Grbavac introduced us to regional flavors—pivca and pura, a true taste of Imotski tradition.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Granada
programme.desc: Malini Angelica explores Granada, in Spain’s Andalucía region, discovering its rich history, vibrant culture, and world-famous charm. She visits the stunning Alhambra, enjoys a delicious tapas tour, and experiences the passion of flamenco. Granada’s blend of Moorish heritage and modern energy makes it one of Spain’s most captivating cities. Special thanks to Gayle for an incredible tapas tour!

programme.title: The Great Greek Myths
programme.desc: Antigone: The Woman Who Said No follows a fearless woman who defies the king’s order to bury her brother. Choosing family and divine law over authority, her act of resistance challenges power and explores themes of justice and loyalty. Discover this powerful tale of courage and conviction.

programme.title: Egypt Yesterday and Today
programme.desc: In this hour-long special, Rick Steves explores the historic and cultural wonders of Egypt, from Cairo and Alexandria to Luxor and Abu Simbel.

programme.title: Concerto - Schubert e Strauss per Mariotti e OSN
programme.desc: The Rai National Symphony Orchestra, under the baton of conductor Michele Mariotti, presents a captivating performance featuring the works of Franz Schubert and Richard Strauss. The programme showcases Schubert's rich melodies and lyrical compositions alongside Strauss's dynamic and expressive pieces, offering a journey through the Romantic and late-Romantic musical landscapes. The orchestra's interpretation highlights the depth and emotional power of these renowned composers, creating a memorable concert experience.

programme.title: Outdoor and Nature EP8
programme.desc: OUTDOOR & Nature, a weekly program that highlights sports, outdoor activities, and the breathtaking scenery of Cyprus, will introduce you to the great outdoors. programme.title: Brač (Supetar)

programme.title: Malini Angelica Marseille
programme.desc: Malini Angelica travels to Marseille, France’s vibrant Mediterranean port city. She explores its historic Old Port, colorful neighborhoods, and bustling markets, discovering the city’s unique blend of French and North African influences. From sampling local dishes like bouillabaisse to soaking in seaside views and street art, Malini uncovers what makes Marseille one of France’s most dynamic and diverse cities.

programme.title: Concerto - OSN Marc Albrecht E Simone Lamsma Torino
programme.desc: In this 2022 concert in Turin, Marc Albrecht conducts the Orchestra Sinfonica Nazionale della Rai, with Simone Lamsma as the violin soloist. The programme includes Shostakovich's Violin Concerto No. 1, showcasing Lamsma's technical brilliance and emotional depth, and Franck's Symphony in D minor, celebrated for its rich harmonies and cyclical structure. The performance highlights a powerful blend of passion and precision.

programme.title: Whats On Italy Music and Motion
programme.desc: Italy’s cultural and creative spirit is showcased through a diverse range of events, from the international prestige of The Venice Film Festival to the craftsmanship celebrated at Salone dell’Alto Artigianato Italiano. Tradition and performance come alive at the Mercantia Festival, while endurance and heritage meet at the Torino Royal Marathon and the Maratona Reale, set against historic royal landscapes.

programme.title: Panel Tokenizing Real Estate
programme.desc: Tokenization is transforming real estate by turning property into digital assets that can be bought, sold, and shared with unprecedented flexibility. This panel explores how blockchain enables fractional ownership, improves liquidity, and opens property investment to a wider, global audience—while addressing the legal, regulatory, and trust challenges shaping the future of tokenized real estate in the Mediterranean and beyond.

programme.title: Makarska
programme.desc: In Makarska, where the Biokovo mountain meets the sea, we explored local flavors with chef Mario Tomaš. On the menu: "utisno" shark, a Venetian-influenced specialty, and njokada—a cinnamon-spiced twist on pašticada.

programme.title: Whats On Italy Music and Motion
programme.desc: Italy’s cultural and creative spirit is showcased through a diverse range of events, from the international prestige of The Venice Film Festival to the craftsmanship celebrated at Salone dell’Alto Artigianato Italiano. Tradition and performance come alive at the Mercantia Festival, while endurance and heritage meet at the Torino Royal Marathon and the Maratona Reale, set against historic royal landscapes.

Take a trip of inspiration, exploration, and adventure with hosts Panicos Pilalis and Aristo Aristeidou. Listen and get motivated to go on your own outdoor adventure in Cyprus!

programme.title: Gold Cuisine
programme.desc: Celebrate rustic baking in this episode, starting with a flavorful olive pie cake and finishing with charming mini olive pies sprinkled with sesame seeds, perfect for sharing or snacking.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: We'll explore flamboyant Sevilla, where orange trees ring lacy palaces, bullfighting is still a cultural event, and little girls dream of growing up to become flamenco dancers.

programme.title: Kaštela (U masliniku)
programme.desc: In Kaštela, a cluster of seven towns near Split, winemaking runs deep. Among olive groves and vineyards, Mladen Kuzmanić treated us to local flavors—sipa i bob, pancetta, goat cheese, and homemade sausage—perfectly paired with a glass of regional wine.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Sicily
programme.desc: Malini Angelica explores Sicily’s enchanting southeast, visiting Catania, Noto, Syracuse, and Ortigia. She dives into Catania’s lively fish market, samples local dishes like pasta alla norma and even horse meat burgers, and enjoys wine tasting in Noto’s vineyards. In Syracuse and Ortigia, she discovers stunning Greek ruins and seaside charm that showcase Sicily’s rich culture and history.

programme.title: Paestum e Velia colonie greche d' Occidente
programme.desc: A journey from Greece to Italy during the archaic era involved diverse human groups settling in Southern Italy, which led to the establishment of Magna Grecia. Discover the archaeological sites of Poseidonia, Paestum, and Elea, Velia, where recent excavations have uncovered ancient temples and artefacts, revealing the region's rich history of cultural exchange and maritime conflicts.

programme.title: 10 Days Estonia
programme.desc: Marco and Erica soak in the vitality of the student town, Tartu, and experience its eccentricities at the Leaning House and the AHHAA Science Centre. Following this, they visit Obinista, the cultural centre of Setomaa, where the Seto community introduces them to the traditional ways of dressing, singing and dancing. They also indulge in a traditional smoke sauna at nearby Võromaa.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: From swimming in the ocean to relishing a traditional Maldivian lobster curry, Kim experiences the true meaning of joie de vivre at Reethi Rah.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Sabrina is in Tiberias, known for its natural hot springs, here she visits the Sea of Galilee. In Nezareth she visits the cave of Mary, ending with Via Dolorosa in Jerusalem.

programme.title: Xplore Czech Republic
programme.desc: Situated on the banks of the Vltava river, Český Krumlov is a fine example of a European medieval town. Rohan witnesses the Gothic, Renaissance and Baroque elements of the stunning State Castle, which dominates the city. He also finds out about the unconventional life and work of the world-famous painter, whose art was once inspired by this fairytale town in mysterious ways.

programme.title: Timeless Tamil Nadu
programme.desc: Ooty has been known to charm visitors ever since British officers used to go there to escape the infamous Indian summer. Sipping tea at the tiered tea plantations, strolling by serene lakes and gushing waterfalls, gawking at gentle deer and majestic elephants at Mudumalai National Park, Alex loses herself in the breathtaking natural beauty of this retreat nestled in the Nilgiri hills.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: After finding his grandparents’ handwritten recipes, Vikramaditya Sodawas, the owner of Karni Fort, decided to revive his family’s prized recipes.

programme.title: 10 Days Estonia
programme.desc: Embracing Estonia’s largest area of swamps, meadows and waterside forests, Soomaa National Park is the second largest national park in the country. While trying out bog walking and camping over here, Marco and Erica experience what is known as the “fifth season” in Estonia, the season of floods. They also explore Tallinn’s history with espionage, and go on to soak in the romance at its Christmas Market.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: Located in the North Malé Atoll of Maldives, the Reethi Rah resort is the perfect combination of island life mixed with world-class luxury.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Sabrina in Jerusalem, takes a guided tour of City of David and visits Western Wall, learning about its significance. Ending with Cardo and Mahane Yehuda Markets.

programme.title: Xplore Czech Republic
programme.desc: With unparalleled old-world charm, Prague is the answer of Czech Republic to Paris. It is considered one of the most beautiful cities in the world. Rohan Patoley learns about the history and architecture of this spellbinding city at sights such as the majestic Prague castle, Wenceslas square and a Jewish cemetery. But the best experience he has in Prague is when he gets to interact with the Prince of Lobkowicz Palace.

programme.title: Timeless Tamil Nadu
programme.desc: Kodaikanal - the name means “The Gift of the Forest”. True to its name, the cozy hill station nestled in Tamil Nadu’s Palani Hills is captivating with its verdant hills, cascading waterfalls, and postcard-perfect lakes. Alex is truly astonished, however, when she stumbles onto an observatory here that has been tracking solar activity for around 115 years!

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: Alex visits Karni Fort and Fort Begu in Rajasthan, where she unearths some prized dishes of the nobilities.

programme.title: Bliss
programme.desc: From hot air balloon rides to visiting cave houses, Kirsty explores the rich geological, cultural and historical heritage of beautiful Cappadocia.

programme.title: Best From The Rest
programme.desc: While Hong Kong may be a haven for social media brags, a closer look at some of its most iconic attractions exposes its authentic richness.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: Shirodhara is a traditional Ayurvedic oil drip treatment that popular world traveler, Jamie Srubis tries at the Mines Wellness Resort in Malaysia. Following this, she tries other treatments such as Urut Melayu, a full body massage which derives its technique from a traditional Malay martial art, and Acupuncture and Moxibustion, the two oldest treatments of Traditional Chinese Medicine.

programme.title: Timeless Tamil Nadu
programme.desc: During her travels across Tamil Nadu, Alex finally reaches one of India’s most famous pilgrimage sites – Madurai. She pays her respects at the nerve center of the city – the Meenakshi Amman Temple. Her exploration of Madurai reveals to her, however, that there is another, rather delightful side to this revered city.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Sabrina visits Ein Gedi oasis and Masada plateau on her way to Eilat, where she visits the beaches, Red Canyon and Wadi Shani.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: Alex visits Karni Fort and Fort Begu in Rajasthan, where she unearths some prized dishes of the nobilities.

programme.title: Bliss
programme.desc: From hot air balloon rides to visiting cave houses, Kirsty explores the rich geological, cultural and historical heritage of beautiful Cappadocia.

programme.title: Best From The Rest
programme.desc: While Hong Kong may be a haven for social media brags, a closer look at some of its most iconic attractions exposes its authentic richness.

She pays her respects at the nerve center of the city – the Meenakshi Amman Temple. Her exploration of Madurai reveals to her, however, that there is another, rather delightful side to this revered city.

programme.title: Backpack
programme.desc: Sabrina visits Ein Gedi oasis and Masada plateau on her way to Eilat, where she visits the beaches, Red Canyon and Wadi Shani.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s adventures in Perth kick off with him immersing himself in the historic grandeur of the Scone Castle, where a mysterious stone tells tales of Scottish monarchs. But the real thunder of the Highlands awaits at the Braemar Games gathering, where tartan meets brawn, and Robbie learns that embracing his Scottish side involves more than just kilts and castles.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Novi Sad, the financial and industrial hub of Serbia.Starting with canoeing on Danube and later exploring the city and its landmarks.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Santa Village at Rovaniemi, where she reads some of the letters that children from around the world write to him.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Luxurious hotels, thrilling water parks, inexpensive food and almost 300 days of bright sunny weather make Bulgaria’s biggest seaside resort, Sunny Beach popular for budget beach getaways. Catriona, however, stumbles onto the historic town of Nessebar, which is over 3000 years old, and realizes that there is more to the place than meets the eye. She goes on to visit the Castra Rubra winery, where she finds out about the history of Viticulture in Bulgaria and the process of wine making.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Benafsha embarks on a whirlwind adventure in Ahmedabad. With just 48 hours in the city, she is determined to make the most of it, but how?

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From exploring a vintage car museum to scaling a 5 meter ice-wall and sampling a rich bread cookie, Watch Alex exploring The Hague, also known as the “International city of peace”.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Požarevac and visits the ancient town of Viminacium. Later visits National Museum, concluding with Majdanpek’s Rajkova Pecina Caves.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Rovaniemi, where she visits a husky farm and finds out about about breeding and raising huskies.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: For the people of Sofia, preserving its heritage is sacrosanct, and this can be seen in the way the city's infrastructure is built around its archeological treasures. Catriona realizes this when she roams around the cobblestone streets of the city. With every monument she visits, she finds herself transported to a different era, right from Perperikon fort, which narrate stories of the Byzantine empire, to Boyana church, which is home to some of the earliest examples of medieval artwork.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: From lazing in hammocks by the beach to sipping drinks on a private yacht, Kim realizes that Maldives is the perfect destination to unwind in style.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying dishes like Stroopwafel and Palat Stoofvlees to visiting the Erasmus bridge, cube houses and wall painting on the streets, Alex explores Rotterdam.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Since medieval times Aberdeenshire and its northwestern neighbour Moray have been the richest and most fertile regions of the Highlands. Aberdeenshire is famed for its Aberdeen Angus beef cattle, its many fine castles and the prosperous 'granite city' of Aberdeen.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Explore Majdanpek with our host as the city brings the viewers closer to the treasures strewn across the Serbian countryside.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Levi is Finland's largest and fastest-growing ski destination. Emeline is here, to find out the exciting activities that Levi offers, in summer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: With snow-capped peaks rising over primeval forests and traditional villages, Paro is like an Oriental Switzerland. On a visit to Paro, Radhika explores famous monasteries such as the Rinpung Dzong and Takstang Monastery, and learns the history and legends associated with them. Along the way, she finds out the secrets to the quintessential Bhutanese peace and contentment.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: An invigorating spa treatment, a cooking lesson, a snorkeling session – Kim has experiences in the Maldives that leave her completely rejuvenated.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying Orange zest beer and traditional Dutch dishes to exploring St.Martin's Cathedral, Dom Tower and enjoying go karting and stand up paddle boat ride, watch Alex enjoy his trip in Utrecht.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: In the enchanting landscapes of Inverness, Robbie’s journey takes a whimsical turn: from eccentric to downright magical.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Nis,one of the oldest cities in Europe.She visits a concentration camp and later paraglides to end by watching a musical performance.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline goes to Ruka-Kuusamo, where she tries river rafting. She also meets a reindeer herder,who tells her about the unique ways of herding reindeer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Actress and travel enthusiast, Radhika Bangia explores the enigma that is Bhutan, when she visits Thimpu, the capital city with no traffic lights and no skyscrapers invading the skyline. Here, she checks out significant sights such as the Tibetan-style National Memorial Chorten, the National Library and the Folk Heritage Museum in order to understand more about the beliefs, history, architecture and cuisine of Bhutan.

programme.title: World Heritage
programme.desc: Explore the ancient heritage sites of Abu Simbel, the Philae complex of Nubia and the Hatshepsut Temple to unravel the mystical stories behind the ancient temples of Egypt.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex tries traditional Dutch food in Leiden, learns about Dutch history at Prinsenhof Museum, visits a Gothic church, a local cheese market and Delftware factory at Gouda.

programme.desc: The grand finale of Robbie’s wild homecoming unfolds amidst the rugged beauty of the Isle of Skye.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Continue exploring Serbia with Heather as she visits the Manasija monastery, Resavska caves, ending with a joyride onboard Eight of Sargan rail line.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: Krisann and Nathan’s vacation in Mauritius begins in Le Morne, where an unexpected adventure awaits the latter.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Known as Druk Yul or the “Land of the Thunder Dragon”, Bhutan is the country of mountains, monasteries and mystery. Radhika Bangia finds this to be true when she visits Punakha, the former capital of Bhutan, where she marvels at the architecture of Punakha Dzong and gets to know the story behind the two rivers, Mo Chhu and Po Chhu. She also explores the sacred temples and Mebar Tsho lake of Bumthang, the religious heart of Bhutan, and gets to know the local legends.

programme.title: World Spas
programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries out spa treatments, which are inspired by the Dead Sea and the desert surroundings at Hotel Kempinski Ishtar in Jordan.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: After Burj Khalifa, Alex has a traditional Emirati platter, visits the gold market and skiing rink, and goes for a camel safari and camping.

programme.title: City break
programme.desc: Nestled in the mighty Alps, tour the Austrian city of Innsbruck with Sabrina and explore a town that’s alpine-urban yet traditional in nature.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: In her final leg of journey, Heather visits the Uvac canyon, tries locally prepared pie, ending with Kopaonik National Park and Jelovarnik waterfalls.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: On an island-hopping adventure in Mauritius, Krisann and Nathan find out that time works differently on these islands.

programme.title: XP Guide
programme.desc: A homely place where the aroma of freshly baked bread greets you as people smoothly glide along on their bicycles. Welcome to Copenhagen.

programme.title: Unwind
programme.desc: Indeya explores the Cinnamon plantations, tries a local dish and witnesses a traditional dance performance.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: Alex has varied experiences in Dubai like trying Lebanese cheese bread, visiting a coffee museum, going to IMG Worlds of Adventure and more.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: In Amsterdam, Alex tries Bitterballen, cannabis cake and visits Heineken Experience brewery. He explores Zaanse Schans village with windmills and Coster Diamonds, the oldest diamond polishing factory.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s journey to reconnect with his Scottish roots begins in Edinburgh, the stunning capital city perched on extinct volcanoes. Navigating the twists of the Royal Mile, strutting around the majestic Edinburgh castle and learning to sip scotch like a true Scotsman, he feels a tad closer to his heritage. The real fun kicks in, however, when he stumbles onto a rather flamboyant way to unleash his inner Highlander.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Like Rome, Plovdiv straddles seven hills; but as Europe’s oldest continuously inhabited city, it’s far more ancient. As Catriona takes a tour of the city to see its iconic attractions such as a Roman Amphitheatre, which was once the setting for gladiator fights, and the striking Baroque Saints Constantine and Helena Church, she begins to see how seamlessly modern life blends with the age-old ruins. Along the way, she realizes why Plovdiv is called the ‘Eternal City’ of Bulgaria.

programme.title: XP Guide
programme.desc: Stockholm, the capital and largest city of Sweden, was built on 14 Islands. This place is also sometimes referred as Venice of the north.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: This exhilarating city of castles, beer and segway rides can take you on a break that will truly leave you catching your breath.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s adventures in Perth kick off with him immersing himself in the historic grandeur of the Scone Castle, where a mysterious stone tells tales of Scottish monarchs. But the real thunder of the Highlands awaits at the Braemar Games gathering, where tartan meets brawn, and Robbie learns that embracing his Scottish side involves more than just kilts and castles.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Novi Sad, the financial and industrial hub of Serbia.Starting with canoeing on Danube and later exploring the city and its landmarks.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Santa Village at Rovaniemi, where she reads some of the letters that children from around the world write to him.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Luxurious hotels, thrilling water parks, inexpensive food and almost 300 days of bright sunny weather make Bulgaria’s biggest seaside resort, Sunny Beach popular for budget beach getaways. Catriona, however, stumbles onto the historic town of Nessebar, which is over 3000 years old, and realizes that there is more to the place than meets the eye. She goes on to visit the Castra Rubra winery, where she finds out about the history of Viticulture in Bulgaria and the process of wine making.

programme.title: City Breaks
programme.desc: Benafsha embarks on a whirlwind adventure in Ahmedabad. With just 48 hours in the city, she is determined to make the most of it, but how?

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From exploring a vintage car museum to scaling a 5 meter ice-wall and sampling a rich bread cookie, Watch Alex exploring The Hague, also known as the “International city of peace”.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Požarevac and visits the ancient town of Viminacium. Later visits National Museum, concluding with Majdanpek’s Rajkova Pecina Caves.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Rovaniemi, where she visits a husky farm and finds out about about breeding and raising huskies.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: For the people of Sofia, preserving its heritage is sacrosanct, and this can be seen in the way the city's infrastructure is built around its archeological treasures. Catriona realizes this when she roams around the cobblestone streets of the city. With every monument she visits, she finds herself transported to a different era, right from Perperikon fort, which narrate stories of the Byzantine empire, to Boyana church, which is home to some of the earliest examples of medieval artwork.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: From lazing in hammocks by the beach to sipping drinks on a private yacht, Kim realizes that Maldives is the perfect destination to unwind in style.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying dishes like Stroopwafel and Palat Stoofvlees to visiting the Erasmus bridge, cube houses and wall painting on the streets, Alex explores Rotterdam.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Since medieval times Aberdeenshire and its northwestern neighbour Moray have been the richest and most fertile regions of the Highlands. Aberdeenshire is famed for its Aberdeen Angus beef cattle, its many fine castles and the prosperous 'granite city' of Aberdeen.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Explore Majdanpek with our host as the city brings the viewers closer to the treasures strewn across the Serbian countryside.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Levi is Finland's largest and fastest-growing ski destination. Emeline is here, to find out the exciting activities that Levi offers, in summer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: With snow-capped peaks rising over primeval forests and traditional villages, Paro is like an Oriental Switzerland. On a visit to Paro, Radhika explores famous monasteries such as the Rinpung Dzong and Takstang Monastery, and learns the history and legends associated with them. Along the way, she finds out the secrets to the quintessential Bhutanese peace and contentment.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: An invigorating spa treatment, a cooking lesson, a snorkeling session – Kim has experiences in the Maldives that leave her completely rejuvenated.

programme.title: Eccellenze Italiane con Piero Muscari intervista a Heinz Beck
programme.desc: Piero Muscari intervista Heinz Beck, chef stellato del ristorante "La Pergola" di Roma. Heinz racconta l'inizio della sua carriera e spiega la sua visione della cucina italiana.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita uno dei ristoranti più raffinati di Georgetown, una elegante cittadina a pochi passi da Washington DC, un ristorante per osservare e per farsi osservare, in inglese si dice "To see and to be seeying". A "Cafe` Milano" Ornella incontra Franco Nushese, che racconta il suo sogno americano. Arrivato da Minori, Franco Nuschese, conquista l' America con l'alta ristorazione e il suo charm Italiano.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado si trova in Piemonte per scorpire il territorio e visitare alcuni ristoranti stellati. In questa puntata Ornella visita la Reggia di Venaria, dove il Re del ristorante "Dolce Stil Novo", chef Alfredo Russo prepara nella spaziosa e super moderna cucina delle prelibatezze tipiche piemontesi. Ornella visita anche la scuola di restauro e parla con la responsabile della scuola Dr.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita Washington DC e va a scoprire un ristorante proprio a due passi dalla Casa Bianca, il ristorante si chiama "Luigino", il proprietario e chef è Carmine Marzano. Chef Marzano prepara delle ricette tipiche piemontesi. Ospite della trasmissione la bellissima Adrianna Sgarlata Miss Virginia 2006. (2006)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita chef Cesare Casella nel suo ristorante nel Village "Maremma" per preparare alcune delle sue ricette preferite. Con Cesare Ornella visita upstate un allevamento di maiali e mucche Chianine. Cesare Casella oltre ad essere un bravissimo chef è anche uno stimatissimo insegnate, è infatti il Dean del programma di cucina italiana all' "International Culinary Center" a Soho ed Ornella è andata a trovarlo con le telecamere di Brindiamo!.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York ed invita i suoi amici chef per preparare insieme delle ricette da gustare durante le festività natalizie. In questa puntata Chef Pino Coladonato, Chef Marcello Russodivito e la giovane pizzaiola Giorgia Caporuscio. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen ospite speciale la soprano Cristina Fontanelli e John La Barbera, oltre a vari ospiti del mondo dello spettacolo, e della moda. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York per la serconda parte di Brindiamo! in my kitchen. I suoi amici chef in questa puntata son lo chef Raffaele Ronca, lo Chef Marco Chirico e il giovane pizzaiolo Ettore Toches. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen la musica è dal vivo con la soprano Cristina Fontanelli ed il compositore Jhon La Barbera, tra gli ospiti di questa puntata il Fashion designer Domenico Vacca. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita cinque amici ristoratori durante le feste Natalizie, prima tappa" Angelina's" a Staten Island, poi e` la volta di "Marco Polo" e "Luzzo" a Brooklyn. A Manhattan nell'Upper East side, Ornella visita "83 e mezza". Ultima tappa per ujn buon gelato,"Dolce Gelateria" nel Village. (2013)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado continua il suo giro per incontrare il suoi amici ristoratori durante le festività di capodanno. Visitando a midtown Chef Pino alla "La Masseria", chef Stefano e Franco a " Viceversa", a "PizzArte" incontra il pizzaiolo Roberto ed il proprietario Dario, a Queens visita "Ornella Trattoria" dove incontra Vincenzo e "Trattoria l'Incontro"dove incontra lo chef e proprietario Rocco. (2013/2014)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: La puntata di oggi è ricca di contenuti. Ornella Fado incontra Cristina Carrani, esperta in materia immobiliare che aiuterà Ornella a scoprire l'Isola di Capri. Dalla moda alla storia, al cibo e all'arte, Brindiamo! Edizione speciale Capri è l'episodio perfetto per festeggiare i 18 anni di Brindiamo! su NYC LIFE.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York ed invita i suoi amici chef per preparare insieme delle ricette da gustare durante le festività natalizie. programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita il ristorante "Lincoln Square Steak House", a due passi da Lincoln Center. In questa puntata Ornella prepara alcune ricette con il Principe Emanule Filiberto di Savoia, gia` ospite dell'evento di Carnevale di alcuni giorni prima, lo chef Marcello Russodivito insegna al Principe come preprare una succulenta bistecca e prepara un piatto dedicato alla Regina Margherita. Questa puntata fa parte della stagione speciale "Brindiamo! International Cuisine". (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita il ristorante di "Angelina's" a Staten Island per preparare alcune ricette con Vincent Malerba proprietario del ristorante, ospite di eccezione la cantante Giada Valenti che oltre a preparare una ricetta con Vincent racconta ad Ornella il suo sogno americano. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado con lo chef Raffaele Ronca, visitano il famoso ristorante giapponese "Sushi Nakasawa", oltre a preprare sushi con Chef Nakasawa, lo chef Raffaele Ronca prepara un piatto a quattro mani con Daisuke Nakasawa e poi un sei mani con Alessandro Borgognone, co- proprietario del ristorante, questa puntata fa parte della stagione speciale "Brindiamo! International Cuisine". (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: In questo epsiodio di Brindiamo! Ornella Fado fa un viaggio dei suoi momenti televisivi. Intervista il famoso chef Joy Campanaro e con chef Ronca preparano gli spaghetti alla chitarra, fa visita a Vincenzo Cutrone allo stabilmento di Brooklyn, "Octopus Garden", presenta una competizione per la regione Basilicata al ristorante Marco Polo, e visita lo chef Marcello Russodivito che festeggia 30 anni del ristorante e conduce un concerto a Suffern NY. (2016)

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Nella prima puntata Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici incontrano il primo gruppo di ballerini.Per essere selezionati per il prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK devono affrontare 3 sfide: presentare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Graham" con il maestro Antonio Fini.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Continuano le audizioni di Antonio Fini e dei suoi giudici per selezionare i migliori danzatori da invitare al prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK. I ragazzi affrontano le tre sfide previste: portare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di Hip hop con Mirco Giordano e Marcello Gargarelli.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Terza ed ultima puntata della serie. Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici sono pronti: chi sceglieranno per volare a NEW YORK per il prestigioso festival della danza? Ancora una volta i ragazzi devono convincere l'importante giuria con il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Parson" con la maestra Abby Silva.

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: cono di stesa, venere nera, e babà al bergamotto. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado introuce il suo amico Geniale Marco Napoli, attore e regista televisivo e cinematografico. Il documentario di oggi vede come protagonista una giovane artista catanese Roberta Finocchiaro "Save lives with the Rhytm.

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1

programme.title: Brindiamo!

Accanto la nonna a visionare tutto. In questo episodio Ornella intervsita un esperto del vino siciliano Pietro Cavallo, e parlano dell'importanza di abbinare il giusto vino ad ogni piatto.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita Gente, dove incontra Jay Mitchel un americano appassionato di Italia al punto di aprire un suo ristorante in america per far apprezzare il cibo Italiano ad i suoi clienti.

programme.title: Brindiamo!

programme.title: Brindiamo!

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado, visita il ristorante "etc.etc". nel cuore di Manhattan per preparare le ricette tipiche del ristorante con lo chef Stefano Terzi. In questa puntata l'ospite è il celebre pianista, Giovanni Allevi, reduce da un favoloso concerto al Carnegie Hall. Ornella intervista Giovanni passeggiando in carrozza a Central Park. (2005)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita uno dei ristoranti più eleganti di Manhattan a due passi da Central Park il "San Domenico ristorante". Ornella Fado indossa alcune creazioni della sua collezione. (2007)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. (2007)

programme.title: Brindiamo!

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita uno dei ristoranti più eleganti di Manhattan a due passi da Central Park il "San Domenico ristorante". Nella cucina del ristorante Ornella assiste Odette Fada nella preparazione dei classici piatti Italiani. Ospite della puntata la giovane stilista Nicole Romano. Ornella Fado indossa alcune creazioni della sua collezione. (2007)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita una delle più belle scuole di cucine upstate in New York la "CIA" Culinary Institute of America, dove visita il ristorante "Caterina dei Medici", e con lo chef Scapin preprano alcune ricette del menù della scuola. (2007)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York ed invita i suoi amici chef per preparare insieme delle ricette da gustare durante le festività natalizie. In questa puntata Chef Pino Coladonato, Chef Marcello Russodivito e la giovane pizzaiola Giorgia Caporuscio. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen ospite speciale la soprano Cristina Fontanelli e John La Barbera, oltre a vari ospiti del mondo dello spettacolo, e della moda. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York per la serconda parte di Brindiamo! in my kitchen. I suoi amici chef in questa puntata son lo chef Raffaele Ronca, lo Chef Marco Chirico e il giovane pizzaiolo Ettore Toches. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen la musica è dal vivo con la soprano Cristina Fontanelli ed il compositore Jhon La Barbera, tra gli ospiti di questa puntata il Fashion designer Domenico Vacca. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita cinque amici ristoratori durante le feste Natalizie, prima tappa" Angelina's" a Staten Island, poi e` la volta di "Marco Polo" e "Luzzo" a Brooklyn. A Manhattan nell'Upper East side, Ornella visita "83 e mezza". Ultima tappa per ujn buon gelato,"Dolce Gelateria" nel Village. (2013)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado continua il suo giro per incontrare il suoi amici ristoratori durante le festività di capodanno. Visitando a midtown Chef Pino alla "La Masseria", chef Stefano e Franco a " Viceversa", a "PizzArte" incontra il pizzaiolo Roberto ed il proprietario Dario, a Queens visita "Ornella Trattoria" dove incontra Vincenzo e "Trattoria l'Incontro"dove incontra lo chef e proprietario Rocco. (2013/2014)

programme.title: Eccellenze Italiane con Piero Muscari intervista a Heinz Beck
programme.desc: Piero Muscari intervista Cesare Berlingeri, noto artista contemporaneo italiano. Cesare illustra l'importanza della sua arte e cosa vuole esprimere con la tecnica dei quadri piegati.

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado introduce il suo amico Geniale Sergio Furnari, e presenta un docuemntario su un suo ultimo lavoro aritistico "Covid Hero Monument"

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado, passa una giornata a New York con il suo amico Geniale, il Maestro Antonio Ciacca, e tra una chiacchierata a Lincoln Center ed una passeggiata a Central Park, arrivano al prestigiosto Pier Hotel dove il Maestro Ciacca si esibisce regolarmente. in questo episodio di "MY TALENTED FRIENDS" scoprirete la storia di successo del Maestro Ciacca in un pccolo documentario MY JAZZ LIFE.

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: portafoglio di squacquerone, ciliegie e mortadella, riso rosso integrale con pollo e peperoni, diplomatico alle rose. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: rotolo di riso, prosciutto e melone, risotto alla rucola con kiwi e vongole, e budino di riso. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Nella prima puntata Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici incontrano il primo gruppo di ballerini.Per essere selezionati per il prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK devono affrontare 3 sfide: presentare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Graham" con il maestro Antonio Fini.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Continuano le audizioni di Antonio Fini e dei suoi giudici per selezionare i migliori danzatori da invitare al prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK. I ragazzi affrontano le tre sfide previste: portare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di Hip hop con Mirco Giordano e Marcello Gargarelli.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Terza ed ultima puntata della serie. Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici sono pronti: chi sceglieranno per volare a NEW YORK per il prestigioso festival della danza? Ancora una volta i ragazzi devono convincere l'importante giuria con il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Parson" con la maestra Abby Silva.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita cinque amici ristoratori durante le feste Natalizie, prima tappa" Angelina's" a Staten Island, poi e` la volta di "Marco Polo" e "Luzzo" a Brooklyn. A Manhattan nell'Upper East side, Ornella visita "83 e mezza". Ultima tappa per ujn buon gelato,"Dolce Gelateria" nel Village. (2013)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita il ristorante "Masseria dei Vini" a New York City per fare un viaggio culinario tramite il buon vino di Puglia insieme a quattro chef della Regione. La prima ricetta la prepara lo chef e proprietario Pino Coladonato, la seconda lo chef Alessandro Pendinelli, la terza ricetta lo chef Massimo Convertini. Per concludere in dolcezza e bellezza il dessert viene preparato dallo chef Mauro Abignente. (2018)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: New York City. Ornella Fado conduce un favoloso evento per la Regione Calabria da "Eataly Downtown", preparando delle ricette con lo chef calabrese Luca Caligiuro, e lo chef italo americano Ryan Rizzuto. Ospite della puntata la chef più amata d'America Lidia Bastianich. (2018)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova a Filadelfia e visita uno dei ristoranti Italiani piu` belli della città, "Gran Caffè l'Aquila". Oltre a preparare una ricetta classica romana con Riccardo Longo, proprietario del ristorante, Ornella prepara una ricetta classica abruzzese con Stefano Biasini. Stefano, "maestro" gelatiere pluripremiato, ci delizia con ottimi gelati. Al bar, Luciano, il bartender confeziona un favoloso ed elegante aperitivo tutto italiano. (2018)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado organizza un favoloso evento al Michelangelo Hotel di New York City, ospite d' eccezione Mara Martini, ristoratrice e chef stellata veneziana. Tra gli ospiti il biologo e star televisiva Jeff Corwin. In questa puntata Ornella incontra il General Manager Maurizio Benevento che racconta la storia del Michelangelo, unico hotel Italiano nel cuore di Manhattan. Ospite Tony Margiotta autore del libro "Hidden Gems of Italy". (2018)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita la Puglia una delle regioni più pittoresche d'Italia per un viaggio indimenticabile. Dai tipici Trulli di Alberobello, alle grotte di Castellana. Rientrata in America, Ornella incontra chef Pino Coladonato al ristorante "La Masseria", per rivivere alcuni momenti del suo favoloso viaggio pugliese, tramite le ricette di chef Coladonato. (2019)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado festeggia il decimo anniversario di "Enoteca Ristorante" e con Chef Marco Chirico preparano piatti originali, tra cui la pizza tiramisù e gustosi cocktail. Ornella e Marco Incontrano Tom Perrotta a Fort Meat, grossista di carne, dove si rifornisce Enoteca Ristorante. Ornella intervista Giuseppe "Jo" Chirico Patron di Marco Polo storico ristorante di Brooklyn ed Enoteca Ristorante.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita il ristorante "Il Circo" nel cuore di Manhattan, dove conosce la signora Egidiana autrice di diversi libri di cucina. Dopo aver preprato alcune specialità del ristorante, Ornella visita il Re della ristorazione francese al ristornate Le Cirque. Il Signore Mauro Maccioni in una intervsita racconta ad Ornella la sua favolosa carriera nella ristorazione.(2007)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita il ristorante toscano "Pepolino" dove prepara delle saporite ricette con lo chef Patrizio, per chiudere in "Dolcezza e Bellezza" Enzo prepara una delle cheese cake piu` famose di New York.(2007).

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: In questa puntata, Ornella Fado conduce un favoloso evento dedicato alla Regione Calabria da Eataly Downtown, preparando ricette insieme allo chef calabrese Luca Caligiuro e allo chef italo-americano Ryan Rizzuto.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Ornella Fado è a Milano, all'EXPO, con i giovani della Basilicata del progetto Sano è Lucano. I prodotti tipici lucani ispirano una serie di incontri culinari: dalla barca alla Mondadori, fino a Lady Bu Bistrò e al ristorante stellato Il Luogo di Aimo e Nadia con lo chef Fabio Pisani.Il racconto è arricchito dagli interventi di Sara Farnetti e Pietro Bitonti.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: In questa puntata, Ornella Fado conduce un favoloso evento dedicato alla Regione Calabria da Eataly Downtown, preparando ricette insieme allo chef calabrese Luca Caligiuro e allo chef italo-americano Ryan Rizzuto.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: La puntata di oggi è ricca di contenuti. Ornella Fado incontra Cristina Carrani, esperta in materia immobiliare che aiuterà Ornella a scoprire l'Isola di Capri. Dalla moda alla storia, al cibo e all'arte, Brindiamo! Edizione speciale Capri è l'episodio perfetto per festeggiare i 18 anni di Brindiamo! su NYC LIFE.

(2013/2014)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova a Rye, New York, e incontra chef Rafele Ronca, proprietario del ristorante "Rafele Rye". La giusta occasione per preparare un menù completo tipico del centro e sud Italia. Ornella prosegue la sua visita intervistando chef Ronca, e l'imprenditrice e filantropa americana Annie Mara, nonchè proprietaria dei New York Giants, squadra della N.F.L. (2020)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado è stata invitata a Staten Island al ristorante "Angelina's" in occasione del fidanzamento in puro stile siciliano di Cristina Malerba. Con Vincent Malerba colgono l'occasione per visionare insieme i preparativi dall'atmosfera siciliana. In cucina con chef Vincent, Ornella prepara alcuni piatti tipici siciliani che verranno poi serviti durante la serata. Incontra poi Angelina Malerba proprietaria del ristorante, e ripercorrono insieme la loro amicizia e carriera.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado nel 2020 festeggia i primi 15 anni di broadcasting su NYC life e Brindiamo! ufficialmente diventa il programma di cucina più longevo del canale. In questa puntata vi presentiamo alcuni dei momenti più belli di questi ultimi anni di produzione alla scoperta della ristorazione italiana ospite della puntata Edoardo Bennato in New York. (2020)
